mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and a lower equity discount rate, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next month. The effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because disinflation preserves the real value of the easing impulse.
failure_condition: Reject if months with declining FEDFUNDS do not produce higher 20d SPX returns than months with flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the signal loses positive rank correlation in disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent trend marks demand deterioration and earnings risk, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as investors price slower consumption and weaker hiring.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE threshold breaches are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous upswing in RSAFS and HOUST indicates stronger household and retail demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and rising HOUST does not outperform either series alone for 60d forward SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO after a prior contraction signals industrial reacceleration and inventory restocking, which supports cyclical earnings and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months after a 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if the specified INDPRO recovery pattern does not produce above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the effect disappears once contemporaneous market volatility is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 and PAYEMS both accelerate, the economy is expanding in real activity and payroll creation at the same time, which boosts earnings expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with joint GDPC1 and PAYEMS acceleration do not show higher 60d SPX returns than quarters with only one series improving, or if the interaction coefficient is unstable across rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level above the neutral-sounding low-rate environment constrains valuation multiples and weighs on equities, but the negative effect is most visible when CPIAUCSL is still elevated, because real rates remain restrictive.
regime_hint: policy tightening or restrictive real-rate regime
failure_condition: Reject if above-threshold FEDFUNDS periods do not underperform on 5d SPX returns, or if the negative effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL is low.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series signals payroll contraction before unemployment fully adjusts, which weakens forward earnings expectations and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month PAYEMS declines are not followed by below-average 20d SPX returns, or if the sign flips positive in more than half of historical trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in RSAFS while HOUST remains weak identifies a demand rollover in both consumption and housing, which transmits to lower S&P 500 returns over the next week through earnings revisions and recession fears.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls below its 3-month moving average and HOUST is below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weak-demand trigger is not followed by negative 5d SPX returns on average, or if either proxy alone explains the effect as well as the interaction.
